lede: A look at January 12 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Artem Kotenko and Miguel Ángel Nieto, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Taal Volcano
events.desc: Taal Volcano in the Philippines erupts and kills 39 people.

events.title: 2010 Haiti earthquake
events.desc: An earthquake in Haiti occurs, killing between 220,000 and 300,000 people and destroying much of the capital Port-au-Prince.

events.title: Comet McNaught
events.desc: Comet C/2006 P1 (McNaught), one of the brightest comets ever observed is at its zenith visible during the day.

events.title: James Bedford
events.desc: Dr. James Bedford becomes the first person to be cryonically preserved with intent of future resuscitation.

events.title: Lyndon B. Johnson
events.desc: Lyndon B. Johnson states that the United States should stay in South Vietnam until Communist aggression there is ended.

events.title: Minnie Pit Disaster
events.desc: The Minnie Pit Disaster coal mining accident occurs in Halmer End, Staffordshire, in which 155 men and boys die.

events.title: Oswald Boelcke
events.desc: Oswald Boelcke and Max Immelmann become the first German aviators to earn the Pour le Mérite, receive the German Empire's highest military award, for achieving eight aerial victories each over Allied aircraft.

events.title: National Trust
events.desc: The National Trust is founded in the United Kingdom.

events.title: John Rennie the Elder
events.desc: John Rennie's scheme to defend St Mary's Church, Reculver, founded in 669, from coastal erosion is abandoned in favour of demolition, despite the church being an exemplar of Anglo-Saxon architecture and sculpture.

events.title: Bayinnaung
events.desc: Bayinnaung, who would go on to assemble the largest empire in the history of Southeast Asia, is crowned King of Burma.

deaths.text: George Dement, American soldier, businessman, and politician
Laurence J. Peter, Canadian-American author and educator
Edward Smith (VC), English lieutenant, Victoria Cross recipient
